source.sourcePath: raw/sources/RA/11_Audit F-up/240415-17 ISO 갱신심사 & 2,3G MDR 심사 - 3EC/MDR 보완/[2nd response TD by H&abyz] Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector (08 March 2024) word/A-TCF-5-155 Risk Management/A-TCF-5-104 Risk Management Report (rev.3).docx

document.title: Risk Management Report for A1717MCW / A1417MCW / F1417MCW

sourceSummary.oneLine: H&abyz risk management report for the Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector models A1717MCW, A1417MCW, and F1417MCW.
sourceSummary.officialUse: Establishes and documents risk management activities in accordance with the risk management procedure for the device life cycle.
keyPoints: The device is a wired or wireless digital flat panel detector for digital radiography systems.
The models are classified as IIb and have an expected service life of approximately 5 years.
Before risk control, 43 risks were identified, with 2 acceptable and 41 unacceptable.
After risk control, all 43 identified residual risks were in the acceptable region.
Production and post-production information is collected and reviewed by Quality Assurance, R&D, Production, Management, and the Quality manager.

evidence.claim: The detectors are wired or wireless digital flat panel detectors for digital radiography systems.
evidence.evidenceText: The A1717MCW/A1417MCW/F1417MCW are wired or wireless digital flat panel detectors that have been designed for faster, more streamlined approach to digital radiography systems.

evidence.claim: The device classification is IIb.
evidence.evidenceText: Classification IIb
evidence.pageTarget: Medical device classification IIb

evidence.claim: The expected service life is approximately 5 years.
evidence.evidenceText: Expected service life Approximately 5 years
evidence.pageTarget: Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector service life

evidence.claim: The device is intended for general radiographic diagnosis of human anatomy for adults and children.
evidence.evidenceText: The A1717MCW/A1417MCW/F1417MCW is indicated for digital imaging solution designed for providing general radiographic diagnosis of human anatomy targeting both adult and children.
evidence.pageTarget: Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector intended purpose

evidence.claim: The device is not to be used for mammography, infants, pregnant women, fluoroscopy, or angiography.
evidence.evidenceText: Not to be used for mammography. Not to be used for infant and pregnant women. Not to be used for fluoroscopy and angiography.
evidence.pageTarget: Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector contraindications

evidence.claim: The device is not life supporting.
evidence.evidenceText: The A1717MCW/A1417MCW/F1417MCW is not a life supporting device.
evidence.pageTarget: Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector safety characteristics

evidence.claim: Patient contact is with the front of the detector for less than 10 minutes.
evidence.evidenceText: Patient contacts the front of the detector in less than 10 minutes.

evidence.claim: The detector can connect to a PC via Wi-Fi and the power supply box can connect by Ethernet cable.
evidence.evidenceText: Power Supply Box can be connected to the PC with an Ethernet Cable. The detector can be connected to a PC via Wi-Fi.
evidence.pageTarget: Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector connectivity

evidence.claim: Before risk control, 43 risks were identified and estimated.
evidence.evidenceText: Through the identification of known and foreseeable hazards, 43 risks were identified and estimated.
evidence.pageTarget: Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector risk evaluation

evidence.claim: Before risk control, 2 risks were acceptable and 41 were unacceptable.
evidence.evidenceText: Acceptable region: 2 ea Unacceptable region: 41 ea
evidence.pageTarget: Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector risk evaluation

evidence.claim: After risk control, all 43 residual risks were in the acceptable region.
evidence.pageTarget: Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector residual risk

evidence.claim: All identified residual risks after control were evaluated in an acceptable region.
evidence.evidenceText: All identified residual risks evaluated after implementation of the risk control measures, are distributed in an acceptable region.
evidence.pageTarget: Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector residual risk

evidence.claim: Overall residual risks were determined acceptable through warning, operating method, adverse event reporting, and usability expert review.
evidence.evidenceText: Finally, the overall residual risks are determined to be acceptable through the warning, operating method and adverse event reporting and usability expert review.
evidence.pageTarget: Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector overall residual risk

reviewFlags.reason: Source metadata authoritySignal is FDA, but the source text lists European Union and Republic of Korea regulations and does not mention FDA.
The report contains inconsistent or likely erroneous standard references, including EN 15223-1 in the standards list and later references to EN 15322-1 or 15322-1.
Text includes wording and transcription issues such as misspellings and malformed phrases, but key metadata and risk conclusions remain readable.
Risk management activities are stated as completed while excluding production and post-production related activities, which may require review for lifecycle completeness.
